description: Component catalog for EXPOZY AI page generation. Contains ONLY component specs, global config, and validation rules. Page definitions are in page_types.json.
page_selection_rules: ONLY include pages the user actually needs. Less is more.
If the user asks for a specific set of pages, generate ONLY those.
Legal pages (cookie_policy, gdpr_policy, terms_conditions) only if the business sells online.
blog_listing + blog_post only if the user mentions blog/news/articles.
userpage only if the business has e-commerce or user accounts.
homepage is always included unless explicitly excluded.
header and footer are handled separately — do NOT include them.

ai_generation_rules.system_role: You are an EXPOZY template generator for the EXPOZY headless e-commerce CMS (Alpine-Expozy-StoreFront).

stack: Global reactive object named `data` (persisted between page transitions)
TailwindCSS utility classes only (no build step; dynamic class generator)
Core API integration via HTML attributes: apiData + keyName + data-* params
SPA routing replaces only <main> content; no full page reload

template_scope.page_template_mode: CRITICAL: Generate ONLY the HTML fragment that will be injected into <main>. Do NOT output <!DOCTYPE>, <html>, <head>, or <body>.
template_scope.header_footer_mode: If generating header/footer templates, output ONLY the header/nav/footer fragment (still no external assets).

framework_functions.routing: Use href('/bg/...') for internal navigation (SPA). Recommended pattern: ...
framework_functions.language_switching: Use changeLang('bg') / changeLang('en') for language switching.
framework_functions.api_calls: Use apiData="get.something" keyName="something" and pass request params via attributes starting with data-.
framework_functions.actions: Use @click="alpineListeners('post.something', $event)" (PLURAL: alpineListeners). Optionally use @success and @error hooks.

output_format.description: CRITICAL: You must output ONLY standard HTML elements. The component catalog below is a BLUEPRINT — not a web component library.
forbidden_patterns: NEVER output <script>, <style>, or <link rel='stylesheet'> in templates
NEVER include CDN assets (tailwindcdn, jsdelivr, cdnjs, etc.) in templates
NEVER output <expozy-*>, <component-*>, or ANY custom/non-standard HTML element tags
NEVER wrap content in tags named after catalog component IDs

how_to_read_catalog.properties: Tells you WHAT content to generate (text, images, data values). Fill with AI-generated content matching the business context.
how_to_read_catalog.structure: Tells you HOW to build the HTML. Dot-notation = CSS classes: 'div.md:flex.gap-6' → <div class="md:flex gap-6">
how_to_read_catalog.structure.elements: Nested element tree using shorthand: '>' = child, '+' = sibling, '[]' = repeat per item, '?' = optional
how_to_read_catalog.endpoints: Which apiData/alpineListeners attributes to place on wrapper elements. Copy ref values exactly.
how_to_read_catalog.ai_fills: Instructions for what content the AI should generate for each property.

example.given: component 'feature_cards' with structure.per_item: 'div.md:flex.md:items-start.md:gap-6' and elements: ['span.p-4.bg-amber-500.rounded-2xl > svg', 'div > h2 + p']
example.correct:
Title here

Description here

styling_rules: Use Tailwind CSS utility classes for ALL styling.
Use FontAwesome 6 classes for icons (fa-solid, fa-regular, fa-brands).
Dark mode support: always include dark: variants for backgrounds and text.
NEVER use inline , , etc.
For star ratings use: — NOT inline
For brand/partner logos, use the brand_logos component with apiData binding — do NOT hardcode SVG logos.
content_rules: All text MUST use vocabulary specific to the business type.
Tailor headings, descriptions, CTAs to the industry.
Write in the language specified in the business context.
Every text field MUST contain actual content — never empty.
ALL testimonials MUST use the testimonials component with apiData and x-for loops — NEVER hardcode static testimonial cards.
ALL brand/partner logos MUST use apiData='get.our_brands' with x-for loop — NEVER hardcode logo images or SVGs.
Content MUST match the business_context. If business_type is 'car service', write about car repairs — NOT generic template text.
security_rules: ABSOLUTELY NO <script> tags anywhere in generated templates.
No vanilla JS DOM manipulation in templates.
No external resources except Google Maps iframe where specified by the catalog.
Images must use /static/images/ prefix or Helpers.image() fallback. All need meaningful alt.
framework_rules: apiData elements must NOT be inside <template> tags (framework restriction).
Use alpineListeners (PLURAL) exactly as the framework defines: alpineListeners('post.carts', $event).
Use @success and @error hooks only as Alpine attributes (no scripts).
For internal navigation links, prefer SPA routing via href('/path') (with href='...' fallback).

common_mistakes.description: CRITICAL: The AI MUST avoid these patterns. Every violation listed here has occurred in past generations and MUST NOT be repeated.

1_no_vanilla_js.rule: NEVER use document.getElementById, document.querySelector, addEventListener, or any vanilla JS DOM manipulation. ALL interactivity MUST use Alpine.js directives.
1_no_vanilla_js.correct: ...

2_no_external_assets_or_full_document.rule: In page templates, NEVER output full HTML documents or external assets. No <!DOCTYPE>, <html>, <head>, <body>, <script>, <link>, <style>, or CDN URLs. The framework loads assets globally.

3_use_alpineListeners_plural.rule: Use alpineListeners (PLURAL). Do NOT use alpineListener (SINGULAR).

4_no_hardcoded_nav.rule: The site_header component MUST use apiData='get.menu' to load navigation dynamically. Only the static home link is hardcoded. All other nav items come from x-for='menu in data.menu'.
4_no_hardcoded_nav.correct: Начало

5_follow_page_sections_exactly.rule: Each page type defines its sections with specific components in a specific order. The AI MUST follow this structure EXACTLY. Do NOT improvise layouts, skip defined components, or add components not listed in the page definition.
applies_to: all page types — check the page_types definition before generating

6_use_catalog_iframe_template.rule: The google_map component defines an iframe_src_template. ALWAYS use it. NEVER use Google Maps embed URLs copied from the web or invented URLs with pb= parameters.

7_complete_all_html.rule: NEVER truncate HTML output. Every opened tag MUST be closed. Every SVG MUST be complete. If approaching token limits, reduce the NUMBER of repeated items rather than truncating HTML.

8_no_script_tags.rule: ABSOLUTELY NO <script> tags anywhere in generated HTML. This includes: mobile menu toggles, year display in footer, counters, animations, DOMContentLoaded handlers. Use Alpine.js directives instead.

9_always_use_section_wrappers.rule: Every content section MUST use an EXPOZY section wrapper from global.section_wrappers. NEVER use plain <div class='container mx-auto'> as a section root.

10_listeners_on_buttons_not_forms.rule: For POST/PUT/DELETE actions, alpineListeners() MUST be triggered from a clickable element (usually a <button type='button'>) using @click. DO NOT put alpineListeners() on <form @submit> or <form @submit.prevent>.
10_listeners_on_buttons_not_forms.correct: <form class="space-y-4">...<button type="button" @click="alpineListeners('User.put_accounts', $event)" class="px-4 py-2 rounded-lg bg-gray-900 text-white">Save changes</button></form>
applies_to: Any alpineListeners() call where the endpoint method is POST/PUT/DELETE (mutations)

11_no_direct_module_calls.rule: NEVER call platform module functions directly in Alpine expressions. ALL platform calls MUST go through alpineListeners(). Direct calls like Gallery.open(url) bypass the event system and will not work.
applies_to: any component calling platform module functions

standard.description: Default section wrapper for most content sections

box.description: Full-width box for embedded content like maps

fullpage_box.description: Full-height box for static content pages (legal, policies)

plain_section.description: Standalone <section> with lock class. No is-section. For newsletter etc.

auth_page.description: Full-screen standalone <section> for auth pages (login, register, forgotten password). Gradient background, centered card layout with min-h-screen. No is-section wrapper.

none.description: No wrapper. Component renders own root element. For global components (nav, footer).

global_components.description: Components on EVERY page, outside per-page section system.

hero_section.name: Hero Section
hero_section.description: Full two-column hero with badge, h1 heading, description paragraph, dual CTA buttons (primary filled + secondary outline), trust indicator chips, and a featured image with gradient glow backdrop. Right column hidden on mobile.

badge.description: Small pill badge above the heading (e.g. '20+ years of experience')

icon_svg.description: Small SVG icon in the badge (e.g. star)

title.description: Main h1 heading, text-4xl md:text-5xl lg:text-6xl

trust_indicators.description: Small checkmark + text chips below buttons

structure.wrapper: Uses standard section wrapper with !py-20 and bg-gradient-to-br from-gray-50 via-white to-gray-50
elements: div.space-y-8 > [div.badge?.inline-flex.rounded-full.bg-gray-900.text-white, h1, p, div.flex.gap-4 > a.primary_cta + a.secondary_cta, div.trust_indicators?.flex.gap-8 > div > svg.checkmark + span]

hero_banner.name: Hero Banner (Simple)
hero_banner.description: Simple heading with optional subtitle and accent bar. For use in two-column layouts paired with image_showcase and feature_cards.

feature_cards.name: Feature Cards (Horizontal)
feature_cards.description: Horizontal flex cards with SVG icon left, text right. For stacking in a column alongside hero_banner.

icon_feature_grid.name: Icon Feature Grid (Centered)
icon_feature_grid.description: Centered feature cards in a responsive grid. Each card has a large circular icon container, heading, and description. Includes section heading and subheading. Cards have hover:bg-gray-50 effect.

icon_svg.description: Stroke-based SVG, w-8 h-8 text-white, inside a w-16 h-16 bg-gray-900 rounded-full container

service_cards.name: Service Cards
service_cards.description: Image-topped cards showing services. Each card has full-width image, heading, description, and 'learn more' link with arrow. Includes section heading and subheading.

structure.wrapper: Uses standard section wrapper with !py-20 and bg-gray-50 overlay
elements: div.grid.md:grid-cols-2.gap-8 > div.bg-white.rounded-lg.shadow-lg.hover:shadow-xl > [img.w-full.h-64.object-cover, div.p-8 > h3.text-2xl.font-bold.mb-4 + p.mb-6 + a.inline-flex.items-center > text + svg.arrow]

stats_counter.name: Stats Counter Bar
stats_counter.description: Dark background section with 4-column grid of large numbers and labels. Used for trust-building metrics like projects count, satisfaction rate, years of experience.

structure.wrapper: Uses standard section wrapper with !py-10 and bg-gray-900 overlay

newsletter_signup.name: Newsletter Signup
newsletter_signup.description: Centered email subscription form with heading, subheading, email input + subscribe button, and privacy note. Submit via alpineListeners('Newsletter.post_newsletter'). Uses plain_section wrapper (standalone <section> with lock class, no is-section).

privacy_note.description: Small text below form (e.g. 'We will never share your email with third parties')

alpine_actions.Newsletter.post_newsletter: Submits email via alpineListeners. Uses options-clear='true' to clear input after success.

cta_section.name: Call-to-Action Section
cta_section.description: Centered CTA block with heading, description, and dual action buttons. Primary button can trigger a modal (via callModal). Secondary is a regular link. Can optionally bind to an endpoint for modal data.

endpoints.description: Only needed if CTA triggers meeting modal. Provides calendar data for the modal.

alpine_actions.callModal: Opens a modal by name. Called as callModal($el) with data-modal attribute on the element.

structure.wrapper: Uses standard section wrapper with !py-20 and bg-gray-50 overlay

required_attributes_conditional.apiData: get.meeting_calendars (only if modal needs calendar data)

meeting_booking_modal.name: Meeting Booking Modal
meeting_booking_modal.description: Full-featured meeting/appointment booking modal. Contains service selector (from meeting_calendars API data), name/email/phone inputs, date picker with available dates, time slot selection, message textarea, and submit. On success, shows a confirmation modal with meeting details and Google Calendar link. Triggered by cta_section via callModal.

success_subtext.default: We have received your request. Below you will find the meeting details.

meeting_booking_modal.data_source: get.meeting_calendars (bound on cta_section, shared via data.meeting_calendars)

booking_modal.visibility: template x-if='data.modals.meeting' > div x-show with backdrop blur

service_select.on_change: Finds calendar by ID, extracts .days into availableDates, shows date picker

date_picker.config: DateHelper.date({dateFormat:'Y-m-d', enable: filtered available dates, minDate:'today', maxDate:'today+29'})

success_modal.description: Confirmation modal shown after successful booking

structure.wrapper: div[x-data with local state] — sits at page level, outside section wrappers
booking_elements: template[x-if='data.modals.meeting'] > div.backdrop > div.modal_card > [div.header.bg-gray-900.text-white, form.grid.grid-cols-2 > select + inputs + date_picker + time_slots + textarea + button]
success_elements: div[x-show='data.modals.meetingSuccess'] > div.modal_card > [header with check icon, content with details, google_calendar_button, close_button]

image_showcase.name: Image Showcase
image_showcase.description: Single featured image with gradient glow. Hidden on mobile, shown on lg+.

brand_logos.name: Brand Logos Bar
brand_logos.description: API-driven grid of partner/trust logos. Data comes from get.our_brands endpoint — NEVER hardcode logos or SVGs.

logos.description: DEPRECATED: logos come from get.our_brands API. Do NOT hardcode logos or SVGs.

brand_logos.ai_fill_instructions: NEVER hardcode brand logos, SVG icons, or placeholder images. ALWAYS use apiData='get.our_brands' keyName='brands' on the wrapper, then x-for='brand in data.brands' to loop. The API provides: brand.title, brand.image.url. AI generates ONLY the section heading text.

gallery_tabs.name: Gallery with Category Tabs
gallery_tabs.description: Tabbed image gallery from API. Tab switching, lightbox.

testimonials.name: Testimonials Grid
testimonials.description: Grid of testimonial cards with stars, quote, avatar, name, role.

blog_sidebar.name: Blog Sidebar
blog_sidebar.description: Sidebar with search, category nav, tag filters. Controls blog_post_grid.

blog_post_grid.name: Blog Post Grid
blog_post_grid.description: Responsive blog card grid with pagination. Filterable by blog_sidebar.

google_map.name: Google Map Embed
google_map.description: Full-width Google Maps iframe. Uses 'box' wrapper.

contact_info_cards.name: Contact Info Cards
contact_info_cards.description: Vertical stack of contact cards with gradient icon, label, value.

static_content_block.name: Static Content Block
static_content_block.description: Full-page static HTML for legal pages. No dynamic data. Uses 'fullpage_box' wrapper.

titled_text_page.structure: div.row > div.column > div.white-background-wrapper > [div.page-title > h1.text-center[style='font-size:32px;'] + p > br, div.text-page]

blog_post_detail.name: Blog Post Detail
blog_post_detail.description: Full blog post view with hero image, article content, social share, meta bar, same-category posts, tags, comment system with star ratings, and sidebar with related posts and newest posts.

post_hero.description: Full-width darkened hero image with gradient overlay, category badge, title, description.

meta_bar.description: Social links (left) and stats (right) — read_time, views, date.

article_body.description: HTML content via x-html with prose classes.

same_category_posts.description: List of posts from same category, auto-fetched via x-init.

comment_form.description: Expandable comment form with star rating widget.

user_dashboard.name: User Dashboard
user_dashboard.description: Full user account page with sidebar tab navigation and 8 content panels.

sidebar.description: User info card + tab nav buttons + logout link.

tab_profile.description: Profile form: email (disabled), phone, first_name, last_name.

tab_invoice.description: Company invoice form with country select, VAT checkbox.

tab_digital.description: Digital product cards with download button.

login_form.name: Login Form
login_form.description: Full-screen centered login card with email/password, submit via User.login, register and forgot password links.

elements: form > [icon inputs, submit button, register + forgot links]

site_header.name: Site Header / Navigation
site_header.description: Global sticky nav with brand, dynamic menu from API, language switcher, CTA, mobile hamburger.

site_footer.name: Site Footer
site_footer.description: Global dark footer with 4-column grid and bottom bar.

structural.required_section_wrapper: Every section must use a wrapper from global.section_wrappers.
structural.x_html_usage: x-html only for CMS content (data.post.description). Never user-submitted.
structural.no_external_resources: Exceptions: Google Maps iframe, Google Calendar URL.

data_binding.all_endpoints_must_match: Only refs in component.endpoints or page.page_endpoints
data_binding.all_data_fields_must_exist: Every x-text, :src, :class must match endpoint returns

error_hints.SEC-001: Remove all <script> tags — use Alpine.js directives instead
error_hints.SEC-003: Use only standard HTML elements from the allowed list
error_hints.SEC-004: Iframes only from maps.google.com, no srcdoc
error_hints.SEC-007: Remove CSS expression()/@import/data: from style attributes
error_hints.SEC-008: Remove <meta http-equiv> — cannot override security headers
error_hints.SEC-010: Form actions must use alpineListeners(), not external URLs
error_hints.ALP-001: Use only allowed Alpine directives/events/bindings from the catalog
error_hints.ALP-002: Use only allowed alpineListeners() endpoints from the catalog
error_hints.ALP-005: Use alpineListeners() instead of direct module calls
error_hints.ALP-007: x-for must be on <template>, not other elements
error_hints.BND-001: Use only apiData endpoints defined in the component catalog
error_hints.BND-002: Add keyName attribute matching the endpoint
error_hints.BND-004: Use dot-notation data paths, not REST /api/ URLs
error_hints.BND-005: Prefix loop source with data. (e.g. data.blogPosts not blogPosts)
error_hints.BND-010: Fix keyName to match expected value for this apiData endpoint
error_hints.BND-011: Fix x-for source to use correct EXPOZY data path
error_hints.BND-012: Unknown x-for source — not a known EXPOZY data path
error_hints.BND-013: Use alpineListeners() for API calls, not custom functions
error_hints.BND-014: Unknown function — use alpineListeners() or EXPOZY module functions
error_hints.BND-015: Wrong state variable in x-data — check platform naming
error_hints.BND-016: Wrong state reference in expression — check platform naming
error_hints.BND-017: Replace vanilla JS DOM manipulation with Alpine directives
error_hints.FLD-001: Loop items are primitives, not objects — remove field access
error_hints.FLD-002: Wrong field name — check endpoint returns schema
error_hints.FLD-003: Unknown field — not in endpoint returns schema
error_hints.HTM-002: Remove unexpected closing tag — no matching open tag
error_hints.HTM-003: Fix nesting — block element inside inline element
error_hints.HTM-006: Add required attribute (e.g. alt on img)
error_hints.STR-002: Add dark: variant for light background/text classes
error_hints.STR-004: Reduce inline SVGs — prefer FontAwesome classes
error_hints.STR-011: Use '!container is-container v2' instead of 'container mx-auto'
error_hints.STR-012: Move background/gradient to .is-overlay-bg child, not section root
error_hints.STR-013: Use dark:text-white or dark:text-slate-300, not dark:text-gray-700+
error_hints.STR-014: Remove CDN assets — platform loads Tailwind/Alpine globally

restricted_html_elements.math: MathML can contain script — verify content
restricted_html_elements.style: Inline <style> can contain CSS injection vectors
